title: Isotoma breviflora
taxonomy: Isotoma breviflora is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Goodeniaceae, which is a family of plants native to Australia and New Zealand. This plant is a member of the genus Isotoma, which consists of around 40 species. The Goodeniaceae family is part of the order Asterales, and the subclass Asteridae. Isotoma breviflora is a relatively small plant with short-lived flowers, and its classification helps to understand its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the same family. Further classification of Isotoma breviflora at the family, genus, and species levels provides a clearer understanding of its unique characteristics and behaviors.
uses: Isotoma breviflora, also known as the blue starflower, has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits. Its leaves and flowers are said to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The plant is also believed to have antimicrobial and antiviral properties, which may help to combat infections and promote wound healing. However, more research is needed to fully confirm the medicinal uses of Isotoma breviflora. It is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any plant-based remedies for medicinal purposes.

side_effects: Isotoma breviflora is a plant species known for its ornamental value, and while it's not commonly used for medicinal purposes, any potential side effects would likely be related to its toxicity or allergic reactions.
